Club Identity Book

The Crown League

An ESSPN production

The six clubs of the Crown League — each a footballing character with its own colours, style and soul. Strength is drawn fresh every season, so any of them can lift the trophy; the identities are forever.

The six clubs

Kingsbridge Athletic

“The Crowns”
Old-money aristocrats

The old-money aristocrats. Kingsbridge carry themselves like a club that's won everything and expects to again — marble halls, a cathedral-sized trophy room, and a support that boos anything short of a title. Elegant, entitled, quietly ruthless. The team everyone loves to beat.

#1A237E #D4AF37
Crest notes

A regal heraldic crest: a royal-navy shield, a gold crown, twin laurel branches, a serif “KA” monogram. Minted gold on deep navy.

Cobalt Bay FC

“The Chrome”
Nouveau-riche project

New money, no history, all glamour. An offshore fortune arrived and bought a galaxy of stars overnight. Flashy, front-footed and financially bulletproof — but does all that chrome hide a soft centre? Rivals adore watching them bottle it.

#00BCD4 #E8F7FB
Crest notes

A sleek chrome-bevelled cyan wordmark with a bay-wave swoosh. Tech-startup slick, a little gaudy — money you can see.

Marlowe City

“The Artisans”
Tiki-taka technicians

Football as art. Marlowe pass you dizzy — a possession-obsessed academy side of slight, silky technicians who’d rather win 4–3 than 1–0 and physically cannot defend a set-piece. Poetry when it clicks; a horror show when it doesn’t.

#2196F3 #FFFFFF
Crest notes

A clean sky-blue roundel: thin line-work, a swirling “M” and ball, lots of white space. Boutique, gallery-poster minimalism.

Ironhaven United

“The Foundry”
Industrial grinders

Forged in a steel town, Ironhaven are grit incarnate — hard, honest, defensively stubborn, the kind of side that out-works you and nicks it 1–0. No superstars, no nonsense, a stand full of dockworkers who’d die for the shirt.

#7B1E2B #9AA7B2
Crest notes

A rugged steel crest: crossed hammers over an anvil, riveted border, bold slab-serif banner. Weathered, heavy, blue-collar — sparks and all.

Meridian FC

“The Nomads”
Cosmopolitan flair

A rootless, glamorous cult club stitched together from every corner of the map — mercurial, expressive, impossible to pin down. A swagger neutrals adore and traditionalists can’t stand. One week sublime, the next a circus. Football’s beautiful drifters.

#C2185B #F5B301
Crest notes

A crimson-and-gold compass-rose roundel with a globe and an ornate “M”. Cosmopolitan, jet-set, premium but rootless.

Sundervale Rovers

“The Terriers”
Small-town scrappers

A tiny market-town side running on heart, duct tape and a manager who’s basically a wizard. All bite and no fear — they’ll throw a punch at anyone on their day and celebrate a scrappy point like a cup final. Everyone’s favourite underdogs.

#FFB300 #2E7D32
Crest notes

A homespun amber-and-green badge: a scrappy terrier’s head, a scarf motif, a hand-drawn “ROVERS” ribbon. Retro non-league charm, lovable.

Crests & copy live in src/assets/logos/ and src/ratings/teams.ts.
